subagent_reports.topic: Design validation: adv-instructions-audit skill (instruction-prose↔anchor audit, distinct from adv-audit spec↔code axis)

sources.label: adv-audit SKILL (analog skill, different axis)
sources.summary: adv-audit is explicitly spec↔implementation: Purpose='compare specs with implementation', Code Mapper builds 'bidirectional spec↔code map' + coverage=mapped/total_requirements, Orphan Detection on source files. No instruction-prose surface (AGENTS.md/project.md/command docs) in scope. Confirms axis separation is clean.

sources.label: repo-instructions-assets.test.ts (CI-order anchor instance)
sources.summary: readFileSync(AGENTS.md, project.md, ci.yml); asserts BOTH agents AND projectContext .toContain('schemas:check → typecheck → lint → format:check → test → build') and ci.yml ordering. Positive control 5(a) is REAL: CI-order prose is anchored.

sources.label: adv-instructions-assets.test.ts (instruction-prose anchor instance)
sources.summary: readFileSync(ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md); asserts presence/absence of section headers, status-marker rows, compression-guard contract tokens. Pure instruction-prose↔assertion anchor. Confirms anchor taxonomy and positions this file as an anchor *instance*, not a thing to re-invent.

sources.label: manifest-doc-drift / checkpoint-surface-drift / skill-loading-policy / commands-spine / adv-stability-docs assets tests
sources.summary: Spot-checked 5 of ~41 *-assets.test.ts (glob returned 41 *-assets files + drift tests). All readFileSync instruction surfaces (.opencode/command/*.md, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md) and assert .toContain/.toMatch on prose tokens. Genuine assertion-based anchors. '~48' is in the right order of magnitude (41 *-assets + several *-drift.test.ts = ~45-48).

sources.label: project.md vs AGENTS.md Development Commands (staleness positive control)
sources.summary: Positive control 5(b) REAL. project.md:50 check chain = 'schemas:check → typecheck → lint → format:check' MISSING check-test-isolation + check-lockfile-policy. project.md:51 build line lacks '&& build:worker'; build:worker absent entirely from project.md. AGENTS.md:41-43 carries all. Ground truth package.json:21 check script literally runs check-test-isolation.ts && check-lockfile-policy.ts → project.md is factually stale.

sources.label: rq-skillProseCompression01 spec (conformance constraint)
sources.summary: MUST: skills follow enforcement-class compression AND 'keep durable invariants in specs, tests, or command contracts rather than standing audit inventories'. Directly governs the new skill: it must be methodology, NOT a standing inventory of anchored claims. adv-audit (134 lines, methodology) is precedent but exceeds 100-line target.

sources.label: External pattern corroboration (executable doc anchors / docs-as-code drift)
sources.summary: Established industry pattern. Livedoc/grund (anchor-resolves-or-fails check), rlm-docsync (spec-first vs reality-first doc-claim evidence), AgentPatterns 'Runnable Documentation' (every prose claim = implicit assertion, promote to CI). 'anchored iff a vitest assertion fails when wrong' matches canonical definition. AgentPatterns warns: prose that PARAPHRASES output → brittle equality assertions → reviewer rubber-stamping = real false-positive risk.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: PASS with minor cautions. (1) AXIS SEPARATION — clean and defensible. adv-audit is spec↔code (skills/adv-audit/SKILL.md:14,79-87: spec↔code map, coverage=mapped/total_requirements, source-file orphan detection). The new skill audits instruction PROSE (AGENTS.md/project.md/ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md/command docs) against vitest ANCHORS. Different inputs (prose surfaces vs spec.json), different anchors (assets/drift tests vs spec requirements), different failure mode (unanchored claim vs unmapped requirement). No overlap. (2) ANCHOR TAXONOMY — sound and machine-checkable (P33). 'anchored iff a vitest assertion fails when the claim is wrong' is operationally testable: the ~41 *-assets + *-drift tests are genuine readFileSync+assert anchors (5 spot-checked, all confirmed). Matches established external pattern. (3) NO RE-INVENTION — design correctly positions adv-instructions-assets.test.ts and the coverage doc (docs/adv-runtime-protocol-coverage.md) as anchor INSTANCES/prior art, not as work to duplicate. The skill is a read methodology over these, not a new test harness. (4) adv-skill-author CONFORMANCE — ≤100-line target is the documented split threshold (adv-skill-author SKILL.md:109-111); 4 rules + 5 dimensions fit if compressed to pointer-line + table form per rq-skillProseCompression01. adv-audit (134 lines) shows methodology skills can run long — the new skill must stay tighter or split to a sibling. (5) DRY-RUN POSITIVE CONTROLS — both real and verifiable in-tree (5a: repo-instructions-assets.test.ts:33-46; 5b: project.md:50-51 stale vs package.json:21 ground truth).

validation.notes: No design-gate blockers. Five validation points PASS with file:line evidence. Three non-blocking cautions: (C1) rq-skillProseCompression01 forbids 'standing audit inventories' — the SKILL.md must be pure methodology (like adv-audit), NOT embed an enumerated list of the ~48 anchors or anchored claims; reference them as a class, generate the list at dry-run time. (C2) ≤100-line cap is real risk: 4 rules + 5 dimensions + frontmatter + positive-control examples will be tight; pre-plan a sibling *.md split (adv-skill-author:109-111) rather than compressing past readability. (C3) FALSE-POSITIVE risk in the anchor taxonomy: a claim can be 'anchored' by an assertion that checks a WEAKER property than the prose asserts (e.g. repo-instructions-assets only checks CI-order substring presence, not the full command matrix — note project.md carries the CI-order line at the level the test checks, yet its dev-command matrix is still stale at package.json:21 granularity). The skill's 'trace-to-anchor' rule must distinguish 'has an anchor' from 'has an anchor that would actually fail for THIS claim's failure mode', else dry-run yields false-anchored verdicts. AgentPatterns corroborates this brittleness risk for paraphrased-prose anchors.

subagent_reports.recommendation: PROCEED to design-gate completion. The design is architecturally sound: distinct axis from adv-audit, machine-checkable anchor definition, correct positioning of existing tests as prior-art instances, both positive controls verified. Before execution, tighten three things in the SKILL.md design: (1) Make 'trace-to-anchor' rule require anchor-FIDELITY not just anchor-PRESENCE — an anchor counts only if a vitest assertion fails for the claim's specific failure mode (prevents the project.md CI-order false-anchored case where substring-presence passes but matrix-granularity is stale). (2) Keep the skill methodology-only per rq-skillProseCompression01 — no embedded anchor inventory; enumerate at dry-run time. (3) Plan ≤100-line compression with a sibling reference file for the 5 dimensions if needed. 'single-source-per-surface' rule is well-motivated by real asymmetry: repo-instructions-assets.test.ts:16 bans count-style staleness ('24 slash-command workflow files') in AGENTS.md but project.md:36 still says '21 slash-command workflow files' unguarded — a concrete drift the skill should surface.
follow_ups: Execution: SKILL.md 'trace-to-anchor' rule must encode anchor FIDELITY (assertion fails for THIS claim's failure mode), not mere anchor presence — primary false-positive guard.
Execution: enforce rq-skillProseCompression01 — methodology-only, no standing inventory of the ~48 anchors; generate list at dry-run.
Execution: pre-plan ≤100-line split (sibling *.md for 5 dimensions) per adv-skill-author:109-111; adv-audit's 134 lines is a cautionary precedent.

Dry-run validity: project.md:36 '21 slash-command workflow files' is an unanchored count-style claim (AGENTS.md equivalent IS banned by repo-instructions-assets.test.ts:16) — good third positive control for the 'single-source-per-surface' + 'test-or-cut' rules beyond the two claimed.

Methodology note: 'runtime-assembly-aware' rule is sound given deploy-local.sh overlay assembly + adv-runtime-protocol-coverage.md — but ensure it doesn't flag intentionally-compressed runtime adv.md text whose invariant is preserved-by-coverage (docs/adv-runtime-protocol-coverage.md:13-28 maps compressed→retained); false-positive vector.

wisdom.content: Anchor presence ≠ anchor fidelity. repo-instructions-assets.test.ts asserts the canonical CI-order SUMMARY string is present across AGENTS.md + project.md, but does not assert the per-command `# check`/`# build` COMMENTS match package.json. Result: project.md passes the anchor while its dev-command comments are stale (missing check-test-isolation/check-lockfile-policy/build:worker). The drift-detector dimension MUST require "assertion fails for THIS claim's failure mode," or dry-runs yield false-anchored (ALIGNED) verdicts over stale prose. This is the bug class that broke CI in PR #172.

proposal.approval_evidence: User confirmed problem + scope ("confirm"). Skill-only + dry-run framing accepted. Out-of-scope explicitly noted: no instruction fixes, no command, no scanner in v1.

Discovery complete: anchor taxonomy locked (the ~48 asset/invariant/drift test files), ad-hoc anchor layer mapped, live drift confirmed (project.md staleness), scope boundaries confirmed (no overlap with adv-audit / adv-instructions-assets.test.ts). Dry-run sample targets locked.

design.approval_evidence: Design validated by independent adv-researcher: PASS (no blockers). 3 cautions folded into design: C3 fidelity rule (drift-detector requires assertion fails for THIS failure mode, not just mentions claim — prevents false-anchored verdicts); C1 methodology-only SKILL.md (no static anchor inventory, rq-skillProseCompression01); C2 pre-plan REFERENCE.md split. Bonus: third positive control added (project.md:36 count-staleness). Axis separation vs adv-audit confirmed clean (spec↔code vs instruction-prose↔anchor).

planning.approval_evidence: User approved objectives/AC + task graph ("approve"). 3-task linear chain. Entering autonomous execution: write skill → dry-run → verify.

Dry-run report: 5 findings, MAJOR_DRIFT, 3 positive controls fired. Governing skill tests 63 passed. Git diff scope clean (1 file only). AC6 live-load deferred to deploy+restart.

acceptance.approval_evidence: Retry after review-matrix propagation. Contract review matrix: 15/15 rows pass/respected, 0 failing (set at 21:15). Executive summary persisted. All AC met.

documents.proposal:
# Proposal: Add `adv-instructions-audit` skill

Ship a read-only methodology skill that audits Advance's instruction prose against executable anchors, then dry-run it against the current corpus as acceptance evidence.

## Core rules (the methodology)
1. **Trace-to-anchor** — every load-bearing instruction claim must trace to an executable anchor: a tool `describe()`, a `manifest.ts` entry, a Zod schema, `FIELD_POLICIES`, a drift-guard test, or the coverage doc. Prose floating free = drift.
2. **Test-or-cut** — load-bearing claim with no anchor → add a drift-guard test OR delete it. No sovereign prose.
3. **Single-source-per-surface** — each surface owns one claim kind (`project.md`=agent context, `AGENTS.md`=dev ramp-up, `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`=full protocol, `command/*.md`=per-gate contract, `specs/`=laws); others point, never restate.
4. **Runtime-assembly-aware** — edits to `.opencode/agents/` + `overlays/` don't reach the live session until `deploy-local.sh --fix` + restart.

## Audit dimensions (mirror adv-audit's shape, different axis)
- **Anchor mapper** — build claim → anchor map across the corpus.
- **Drift detector** — prose vs anchor mismatch (the CI-order class).
- **Coverage checker** — which invariants have anchors/tests; generalize the coverage doc beyond runtime `adv.md`.
- **Duplication detector** — same claim restated across surfaces.
- **Orphan detector** — prose with no anchor and no load-bearing role.

## Scope of THIS change
- Deliver: `skills/adv-instructions-audit/SKILL.md` (follows `adv-skill-author` format + `rq-skillProseCompression01`).
- Acceptance: dry-run report applying the skill to Advance's own corpus — must catch ≥1 real drift class (positive control: the CI-order anchor) and produce actionable findings.
- Out of scope: any instruction *fixes* (dry-run only — follow-up changes), a companion `/adv-instructions-audit` command, deterministic scanner scripts.

## Why a skill, not a command/tool
Dry-run-first: validate the methodology produces useful findings before investing in command wiring or a scanner. If the dry run lands well, a follow-up change can promote it to a command + scanner (same path `adv-audit` and `slop-scan` took).
documents.problemStatement:
# Problem

Advance ships an instruction surface of ~10 cooperating artifacts — `project.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, 27 `.opencode/command/*.md` contracts, `.opencode/agents/*.md` prompts, `overlays/`, `skills/`, `.adv/specs/`, and `docs/adv-runtime-protocol-coverage.md`. Most load-bearing claims in these files are **prose with no executable anchor**: not tied to a tool `describe()`, a `manifest.ts` entry, a Zod schema, or a drift-guard test.

**Failure mode:** prose drifts from executable contracts and is caught reactively, often mid-PR. Concrete instance this week — `AGENTS.md`'s CI-order line is enforced by a shared canonical string in `repo-instructions-assets.test.ts` (asserted across `AGENTS.md` + `project.md`); a docs edit treated the prose as sovereign and broke CI.

**What exists:**
- `adv-audit` audits **spec ↔ implementation** drift. It does NOT cover **instruction-prose ↔ executable-anchor** drift.
- `docs/adv-runtime-protocol-coverage.md` is a coverage matrix — but only for the runtime `adv.md` agent, not the full corpus.
- Drift-guard tests exist ad hoc (`repo-instructions-assets`, `no-psw-references`, `spec-citation-invariant`, `workflow-bundle-boundary`, `context-snapshot.purity`, `deploy-local`). No methodology says "every load-bearing instruction claim must trace to one of these, else it's drift on a timer."

**Gap:** no read-only audit that maps instruction claims → anchors, flags unanchored/contradictory/duplicated prose, and applies **test-or-cut** (load-bearing + unanchored → add a test or delete the claim).
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives
1. Ship `skills/adv-instructions-audit/SKILL.md` — a read-only methodology that audits Advance's instruction prose against executable anchors.
2. Dry-run apply the skill to Advance's own instruction corpus; produce a findings report as acceptance evidence.
3. Skill conforms to `adv-skill-author` format + `rq-skillProseCompression01` / Prose-Load Reduction Rules.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `skills/adv-instructions-audit/SKILL.md` exists; SKILL.md ≤100 lines; valid frontmatter (`name`, `description` with "Use when…", ≤1024 chars, third person). Passes the `adv-skill-author` review checklist.
2. Skill encodes the **4 core rules** (trace-to-anchor, test-or-cut, single-source-per-surface, runtime-assembly-aware) and **5 audit dimensions** (anchor mapper, drift detector, coverage checker, duplication detector, orphan detector), with the concrete anchor taxonomy (the ~48 `*-assets.test.ts` / invariant / drift test files).
3. A **dry-run findings report** is produced as an acceptance artifact; it catches ≥1 real drift class, including the positive control (CI-order prose → `repo-instructions-assets.test.ts` anchor, and the live `project.md` ↔ `AGENTS.md` dev-commands duplication drift).
4. **Dry-run is read-only:** no instruction file is modified by this change (all fixes are out of scope / follow-up).
5. No new `/adv-instructions-audit` command, ADV tool, or deterministic scanner ships in v1.
6. After `deploy-local.sh --fix`, the skill appears in the runtime `available_skills` list.

## Constraints
- Read-only methodology; never auto-fix (mirrors `adv-audit` constraint).
- Different axis from `adv-audit` (instruction-prose↔anchor, not spec↔code) — no functional overlap.
- SKILL.md ≤100 lines; deeper content → sibling `*.md` (progressive disclosure per `adv-skill-author`).
- Implementation in the per-change worktree (trunk write firewall).
- No new spec in v1 (instruction auditing is not currently a spec'd capability; no spec-citation obligation).

## Avoidances
- Don't ship a companion command or deterministic scanner in v1 (dry-run-first; promote later if output earns it).
- Don't fix any instruction drift surfaced by the dry run — separate follow-up change(s).
- Don't assert unverified claims about external/tool surfaces (P34).
- Don't silently expand into spec-law changes.
documents.design:
# Design: `adv-instructions-audit` skill

## Approach

A read-only methodology skill that treats Advance's ~48 `*-assets.test.ts` / invariant / drift test files as the **anchor layer** for instruction prose, and audits instruction surfaces against it. The skill is the *meta-layer* that makes the existing ad-hoc anchor layer systematic and discoverable; it adds no new enforcement. Dry-run-first: a manual application produces a findings report; no fixes ship in v1.

Mirrors `adv-audit`'s shape (dimensions + health synthesis + read-only constraint) on a **different axis** (instruction-prose↔anchor, not spec↔code).

## LBP decisions
- **Skill-only in v1, not a command/scanner.** Validate methodology value via dry run before investing in `/adv-instructions-audit` wiring or a deterministic scanner. Reversible: `adv-audit` and `slop-scan` both started as skills, then gained commands.
- **Anchor = a failing assertion for THIS failure mode.** Per validator C3: an anchor is not "a test that mentions the claim" — it is "a vitest assertion that fails when *this specific claim* is wrong." Paraphrased/substring anchors are brittle (false-anchored verdicts). The drift-detector dimension enforces fidelity, not just presence.
- **Reuses the existing coverage-doc pattern** rather than inventing a new matrix — the coverage dimension generalizes `docs/adv-runtime-protocol-coverage.md` beyond runtime `adv.md`.

## SKILL.md structure (target ≤100 lines, methodology-only)

Per `rq-skillProseCompression01` / `advance-meta.md:714` (validator C1): SKILL.md is **methodology only**. It must NEVER embed a static inventory of the ~48 anchor tests — those are enumerated at dry-run time via `rg`. Pre-plan a sibling `REFERENCE.md` split if any of the 5 dimensions can't fit inline (validator C2).

```
skills/adv-instructions-audit/SKILL.md
---
name: adv-instructions-audit
description: <third-person, "Use when…", ≤1024 chars>
keywords/metadata: (mirror adv-audit frontmatter style)
---

# ADV Instructions Audit
## Purpose            — audit instruction prose vs executable anchors
## When to use        — triggers (instruction edits, drift suspicion, coverage gaps)
## Core rules         — trace-to-anchor / test-or-cut / single-source-per-surface / runtime-assembly-aware
## Anchor taxonomy    — the 5 anchor KINDS + the rg command to enumerate them at runtime (no static list)
## Audit dimensions   — anchor mapper / drift detector (fidelity) / coverage checker / duplication detector / orphan detector (table)
## Findings & health  — finding schema + ALIGNED/DRIFT_DETECTED/MAJOR_DRIFT (mirror adv-audit)
## Dry-run procedure  — pick surfaces → map claims → classify by fidelity → report (no fixes)
## Constraints        — read-only; no auto-fix; different axis from adv-audit; methodology-only (no static inventories)
```

## Anchor taxonomy (kinds only — enumerated at runtime, not embedded)

| Anchor kind | Enumerate at runtime via | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Surface-asset | `rg -l 'AGENTS\.md\|ADV_INSTRUCTIONS\.md\|project\.md' plugin/src/**/*.test.ts` | `repo-instructions-assets.test.ts`, `adv-instructions-assets.test.ts` |
| Invariant | filename match `*invariant*` / `*no-psw*` | `spec-citation-invariant.test.ts` |
| Boundary | `workflow-bundle-boundary`, `context-snapshot.purity` | layer-import guards |
| Per-command contract | filename match `adv-<cmd>-assets.test.ts` | `adv-task-assets.test.ts` |
| Drift | filename match `*drift*` | `manifest-doc-drift`, `checkpoint-surface-drift` |

A claim is **anchored** iff one of these asserts it AND the assertion fails for that claim's specific failure mode (fidelity, not just presence).

## Audit dimensions → finding schema

Each finding: `{ id, surface, claim, dimension, anchor (or null), fidelity (pass/fail/n/a), severity, fix }`.

| Dimension | Detects | Severity default |
|---|---|---|
| Anchor mapper | claim with no matching test | REVIEW |
| Drift detector (**fidelity**) | prose contradicts its anchor, OR anchor only mentions the claim without failing for its failure mode | HIGH |
| Coverage checker | invariant with no anchor anywhere (coverage-doc gap) | MEDIUM |
| Duplication detector | same claim restated across surfaces (project.md↔AGENTS.md) | MEDIUM |
| Orphan detector | prose with no anchor and no load-bearing role | LOW |

Health: `ALIGNED` / `DRIFT_DETECTED` (any unanchored/dup/orphan) / `MAJOR_DRIFT` (any HIGH drift). Mirrors `adv-audit`.

## Dry-run procedure (the acceptance artifact)
1. Sample 3 surfaces: `project.md`, `AGENTS.md`, one `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` section.
2. Enumerate the anchor layer at runtime (`rg`); do NOT use a baked list.
3. For each load-bearing claim, map to an anchor test AND assess fidelity (does the assertion fail for this claim's failure mode?).
4. Run the 5 dimensions; emit findings.
5. **Three positive controls must fire:**
   - (a) CI-order prose → `repo-instructions-assets.test.ts` (anchored, fidelity-pass).
   - (b) `project.md` dev-commands matrix staleness vs `AGENTS.md`/`package.json` (duplication + drift; the CI-order *substring* anchor is presence-only and does NOT catch the matrix staleness — demonstrates the fidelity rule).
   - (c) `project.md:36` "21 slash-command workflow files" — unanchored count-staleness (test-or-cut / single-source-per-surface).
6. Produce report; **modify no files** (AC4).

## Scope boundaries (no overlap)
- `adv-audit` = spec↔code. This skill = instruction-prose↔anchor. Different inputs, anchors, failure modes. (Validator-confirmed.)
- `adv-instructions-assets.test.ts` = an anchor *instance* (one of ~48), not a competitor.
- `docs/adv-runtime-protocol-coverage.md` = the coverage dimension's prior art, scoped to runtime `adv.md` only.

## Validator-driven refinements (incorporated)
- **C3 → drift-detector enforces fidelity**, not presence. Positive control (b) deliberately demonstrates the gap a presence-only check misses.
- **C1 → SKILL.md is methodology-only**; no static anchor inventory (rq-skillProseCompression01 / advance-meta.md:714).
- **C2 → pre-plan REFERENCE.md split** if dimensions exceed the ≤100-line budget.
- **Bonus → third positive control** (project.md:36 count-staleness) added.

## Verification plan
- AC1/2: review against `adv-skill-author` checklist; confirm 4 rules + 5 dimensions + fidelity rule present; SKILL.md methodology-only, ≤100 lines.
- AC3: dry-run report catches all three positive controls.
- AC4: `git diff` shows only the new skill file (+ sibling if any); no instruction-prose edits.
- AC5: no new entries in `.opencode/command/`, `plugin/src/tools/`, or scanner scripts.
- AC6: `scripts/deploy-local.sh --check` then confirm skill in `available_skills`.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

Shipped `skills/adv-instructions-audit/SKILL.md` — a read-only methodology that audits Advance's instruction prose against its executable anchors (the ~47 `*-assets.test.ts` / invariant / drift test files). The skill is the meta-layer that makes Advance's existing ad-hoc anchor layer systematic and discoverable; it adds no enforcement and occupies a distinct axis from `adv-audit` (instruction-prose↔anchor vs spec↔code).

**Core rules:** trace-to-anchor *with fidelity* (an anchor must fail for the claim's specific failure mode — presence alone is a false anchor), test-or-cut, single-source-per-surface, runtime-assembly-aware. **Five dimensions:** anchor mapper, drift detector (fidelity), coverage checker, duplication detector, orphan detector.

**Validated outcome (dry-run):** applied to `project.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and one `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` section. Produced 5 findings (2 HIGH, 3 MEDIUM), health MAJOR_DRIFT. All three positive controls fired — most importantly, the methodology **reproduced the exact bug class that broke CI in PR #172**: a presence-only anchor (the canonical CI-order string) passed while the per-command dev-comments drifted from `package.json`. The fidelity rule is what made that surface; without it, the two HIGH findings would be falsely rated "anchored."

**Verification:** 63 governing skill tests pass (loading-policy, skill-backed-commands, repo-instructions-assets); git diff vs trunk is exactly one file; no command/tool/scanner added; AC6 live-load deferred to deploy+restart per the source-vs-dist gotcha.

**Follow-ups (out of scope):** (1) fix project.md dev-commands + drop the stale "21" count; (2) add a drift-guard tying dev-command comments to package.json (closes the class that bit PR #172); (3) extend the coverage doc beyond runtime adv.md. Bonus: deploy --check surfaced a pre-existing `adv_change_forget` tool-drift the methodology would also catch.

**Outcome:** the skill earns its keep — it turned a reactive, mid-PR drift failure into a minutes-long, structured audit with actionable findings and a correct root-cause diagnosis.
